channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NBI arrests suspect, confiscates over $200 million in counterfeit money and bonds
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) recently seized more than $200 million worth of counterfeit cash and US treasury bonds. The seized money included fake US dollars and Malaysian ringgit. One ...

item.title: KRA Introduces AI Tool to Improve Tax Compliance and Analyse Data from Multiple Sources
item.description: The Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) is set to introduce a new Intelligence Analysis Tool (IAT) aimed at improving tax compliance and revenue collection. This system will help the taxman collect and anal...

item.title: Thousands of white South African expats returning home, citing better quality of life and rejecting persecution claims
item.description: A growing number of white South Africans who emigrated abroad are now choosing to return to South Africa. They point to factors such as lower living costs, strong family connections, and instability i...

item.title: Zandi Nhlapo promotes open conversations about menopause and women’s well-being
item.description: South African television personality Zandi Nhlapo is encouraging women to have more open conversations around menopause and ageing, highlighting a need for better education and emotional support. Spea...

item.title: Pakistan Facilitates Dialogue Among Regional Capitals Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Islamabad is actively positioning itself as a mediator in the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, following attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel. Pakistan strongly condemns the attacks on ...

item.title: NLC Calls For Audit Of N20bn Transmission Company Contracts
item.description: The Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) don express serious concern say some contracts within the Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) fit as high as N20 billion dey questionable. According to NLC, some of...

item.title: Beyond Funds: Key Factors for Successful Devolution in Kenya
item.description: Devolution in Kenya has brought significant funds to counties, but success demands more than just money. The article argues that while billions of shillings have been allocated to the 47 counties sinc...

item.title: Election Commission appoints senior officers as returning officers for West Bengal assembly polls
item.description: In an unprecedented move for West Bengal, the Election Commission of India (ECI) has appointed officers of Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) level and above as returning officers (ROs) for all 294 assem...

item.title: Rising Costs Threaten Access to School Excursions in NSW
item.description: A Sydney parent and long-time P&C member has highlighted the growing financial barriers to public school excursions, using his year 5 son's potential Canberra trip as an example. Increasing costs mean...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs co-coach Cedric Kaze targets top-three finish amid pressure and recent poor form
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs co-coach Cedric Kaze has boldly claimed that securing a top-three finish in the Betway Premiership this season would rank him and his colleague Khalil Ben Youssef as the most successful ...

item.title: B.C. First Nations chiefs respond sarcastically to MP Aaron Gunn's criticism of land acknowledgments
item.description: The chiefs of four Vancouver Island First Nations—Tla’amin, Homalco, K’omoks, and Klahoose—have issued a humorous yet firm joint statement addressing comments made by Conservative MP Aaron Gunn regard...

item.title: Montreux Jazz Festival Franschhoek Set for Same Weekend as Cape Town Jazz Festival
item.description: This year, the Cape Town International Jazz Festival (CTIJF), a cornerstone of South Africa's music calendar for over 25 years, faces an unexpected challenge. A new international festival, the Montreu...

item.title: Nicole Kidman Asked Co-Star to Avoid Falafel Before Filming Kissing Scenes
item.description: Nicole Kidman shared a humorous behind-the-scenes moment from the filming of HBO’s acclaimed series Big Little Lies. During an appearance on the podcast Las Culturistas, Kidman revealed that she once ...

item.title: UK-wide police operation targets county lines drug networks, arrests over 2,000 suspects
item.description: In a nationwide crackdown on 'county lines' drug networks, police forces across the United Kingdom carried out coordinated raids leading to the arrest of 2,180 people and the safeguarding of nearly 80...

item.title: Former ISU President Aleth Misola Mamauag Acquitted in Fairview Property Dispute
item.description: The Sandiganbayan, the Philippines' anti-graft court, has acquitted former Isabela State University (ISU) president Aleth Misola Mamauag of graft charges related to the university's 2012 property acqu...

item.title: Senator Akpabio Celebrates Nigerian Women on International Women’s Day
item.description: The President of the Senate, Senator Godswill Akpabio, don hala praise for Nigerian women as e mark International Women’s Day 2026. For statement wey him Special Adviser on Media and Publicity, Hon. E...

item.title: Trends and Challenges in the UK Sheep Industry
item.description: The traditional image of the British countryside, with rolling green hills and flocks of sheep, is changing as the UK sheep population declines. As of June 2025, the total number of sheep and lambs fe...

item.title: Indian Stock Market Opens Lower as Global Oil Prices Surge
item.description: The Indian stock market is bracing for another negative opening, with both the Sensex and Nifty expected to fall due to a sudden shock in global oil prices. Analysts are closely watching how rising cr...

item.title: Indian Study Reveals High Screen Time and Addiction Risks Among Children with Mental Disorders
item.description: A recent study published in the Indian Journal of Psychiatry has raised serious concerns about screen media habits among children and adolescents in India, particularly those attending psychiatric ser...

item.title: Tanzania Focuses on Domestic Revenue to Fund Sh62.3 Trillion 2026/27 Budget
item.description: Dodoma – The Tanzanian government is planning to rely more on domestic revenue to fund its 2026/27 national budget, which is set at Sh62.3 trillion. This move comes after some donor countries indicate...
